variation type: Nucleotide variant detected in multi-copy gene
evidence code: ECO:0001113 point mutation phenotypic evidence, ECO:0001103 natural variation mutant evidence
evidence limitations: lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: Acquired mutation in 23s rRNA encoding gene confers resistance to macrolides. Bordetella pertussis has 3 copies of 23S rRNA. Mutation in all 3 copies is required to confer resistance. Mutation in one or two copies leads to heterogeneous pattern of resistance. Mutation is equivalent to 23S_A2058G (NC_004431.1) in E. coli. In the original publication (PMID:12624047), this mutation was referred to as 2047A>G because the relative position was based on an old sequence of the B. pertussis 23S rRNA  that has been discontinued in refseq/genbank. The relative position of this mutation in the current B. pertussis 23S sequence (NR_103973.1) is 2037A>G and this is the nomenclature we have used. Most papers use the original 2047A>G nomenclature to be consistent as explained in PMID: 30898080.

evidence limitations: statistical geno/pheno evidence but no experimental evidence, lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: blaTEM-1 has been found in B. bronchiseptica strains displaying resistance to ceftiofur with MIC >8 mg/L in PMID: 19914850. blaTEM-1 in B. bronchiseptica has been detected in both plasmids and on the chromosome.

evidence limitations: statistical geno/pheno evidence but no experimental evidence
rule curation note: flor2 was detected in 9/18 (50%) B. bronchiseptica strains displaying resistance to florfenicol (MIC >8 mg/L) in PMID: 17224413.

evidence limitations: lacks evidence for this genus, lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: sul1 has been found in in B. bronchiseptica strains displaying a wide range of MICs to trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole. In other genera, sul1 typically confers resistance to sulfonamides. While there are no breakpoints for this species, B. bronchiseptica isolates carrying sul1 had trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole MICs between <=0.015/0.3 and 2/38 mg/L (PMID: 26275219) which supports the same interpretation in this species.

evidence limitations: lacks evidence of the degree to which MIC is affected, lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: sul1 typically confers resistance to sulfonamides in other species. In B. bronchiseptica,  while there are no breakpoints, sul2 had a statistical significant association with resistance to  trimethoprim / sulfamethoxazole when MIC >2 mg/L was used as a cutoff (PMID:39669782). In another study, isolates containing sul2 only had a wide range of  trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole MICs between 0.03/0.6 to 8/152 mg/L (PMID: 26275219). Therefore, the available evidene also supports sul2 conferring resistance to sulfonamides in B. bronchiseptica.

evidence limitations: statistical geno/pheno evidence but no experimental evidence
rule curation note: PMID:26275219 refers to aph(3'')-Ib as strA. aph(3'')-Ib was detected in 17/18 B. bronchiseptica isolates with streptomycin MIC >=1024 mg/L (PMID: 26275219).

evidence limitations: statistical geno/pheno evidence but no experimental evidence
rule curation note: PMID:26275219 refers to aph(6)-Id as strB. aph(6)-Id was detected in 17/18 B. bronchiseptica isolates with streptomycin MIC >=1024 mg/L (PMID: 26275219).

evidence limitations: lacks evidence for this genus, lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: tet(G) identified in 4/103 B. bronchseptica genomes screened using AMRfinderPlus. tet(G) confers tetracycline resistance in other genus but there is no direct evidence for this in B. bronchiseptica yet.

evidence limitations: statistical geno/pheno evidence but no experimental evidence, lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: B. bronchiseptica strains carrying dfrA1 also carry sul1. No B. bronchiseptica strains carrying dfrA1 only has been reported in the literature to date. It is assumed that resistance to trimethroprim in strains carrying dfrA1-sul1 (PMID: 16046466) is due to dfrA1 based on results in other genus.

evidence limitations: lacks evidence of the degree to which MIC is affected, lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: B. bronchiseptica strains carrying dfrA7 also carry sul1. dfrA7 in combination with sul1 confers resistance to trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole. The degree to which dfrA7 affects trimethoprim MIC alone is unknown as this has not been investigated.

evidence limitations: statistical geno/pheno evidence but no experimental evidence, lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: B. bronchiseptica strains carrying dfrB1 also carry sul1. No B. bronchiseptica strains carrying dfrB1 only has been reported in the literature to date.  It is assumed that resistance to trimethroprim in strains carrying dfrA1-sul1 (PMID: 16046466) is due to dfrA1 based on results in other genus.

rule curation note: catB3 was detected in 5/6 B. bronciseptica strains carrying pKBB668 plasmid with resistance to chloramphenicol (MIC >=16 mg/L) in PMID: 16046466. Mobilisation of plasmid pKBB668 into E. coli conferred resistance to chloramphenicol.

evidence code: ECO:0001103 natural variation mutant evidence, ECO:0005027 genetic transformation evidence
evidence limitations: lacks formal breakpoints
rule curation note: cmlB1 was detected in a B. bronchiseptica isolate with chloramphenicol MIC of 128 mg/L. Mobilisation of cmlB1 showed up to 16-fold increase in chloramphenicol MIC in B. bronchiseptica and E. coli transformants.

evidence code: ECO:0001103 natural variation mutant evidence, ECO:0005027 genetic transformation evidence
evidence limitations: statistical geno/pheno evidence but no experimental evidence
rule curation note: Acquired gene expected to confer high-level resistance to penicillins. blaOXA-2 was detected in 13 isolates showing elevated MIC to ampicillin (16-128 mg/L) in PMID: 26275219 and 17261565. Mobilisation of plasmid pKBB282 containing blaOXA-2 conferred ampicillin resistance in E. coli and B. bronchiseptica. The presence of blaOXA-2 in addition to the species-specific blaBor-1 was suggested to further increase MIC of ampicillin.

evidence code: ECO:0000005 enzymatic activity assay evidence, ECO:0005027 genetic transformation evidence
evidence limitations: lacks evidence of the degree to which MIC is affected
rule curation note: Wildtype core gene expected to confer resistance to penicillins. Although there are no EUCAST or CLSI human clinical breakpoints for penicillins in B. bronchiseptica and no expected resistance recorded by EUCAST, there are CLSI veterinary breakpoints and by this standard B. bronchiseptica have expected resistance which evidence (PMID:15917575) suggests is due to this gene. Additionally, while there is no formal ECOFF, the EUCAST ampicillin MIC breakpoint distribution suggests a median MIC of 8 mg/L, consistent with expected resistance.
